evidence: Ivor: 'The value they get in the first six months will be like... yeah. The value they'll have in six years time if they keep up with it will be wild. Those people won't just be users, they'll be like fans and advocates.'
Longitudinal career data requires 6+ year patience before peak value - compounding relationship over transaction
V: 'Academies have their local towns, regions, catchment areas locked the fuck down. And that is why you can reliably generate talent. If you aren't able to do that, you don't survive as an academy.'
Implies territorial monopoly model vs. database/sourcing model - 'Companies don't recruit; they cultivate from early stages'
V: 'Companies will never invest in true AI for resume scanning. Because why would you process a resource where the ratio is like 99 crap for 1% signal. And even that could be totally fake now.'
Information asymmetry is increasing, not decreasing - the ATS is a permanent ceiling, not a problem to solve
AI-generated applications make traditional assessment structurally broken - 'even that could be totally fake now'
V: 'Handshake is literally what I think is the actual perfect acquirer' - Ivor to facilitate introduction via new Head of Strategic Ops
Ivor's new Head of Strategic Ops will reach out to Handshake after joining week after next

source_display: The Quiet Part Problem as Market Opportunity
evidence: Employers have selection criteria they cannot legally state - the 'silent scan' bridges this gap through behavioral/cultural signals without legal exposure

source_display: Talent Scanning as Core Value Proposition
evidence: V: 'What we are is talent scanning, and that's literally the only thing that matters' - fundamental pivot from previous positioning

source_display: Response Rate as the Only Metric That Matters
evidence: Logan's insight: use response rates as the convincing proxy - 'our users pay attention to our recommendations'

source_display: Response Rate as the Only Metric That Matters
evidence: Response rate measures candidate intent, not employer efficiency - inverts standard recruiting metrics (volume, match quality, time-to-fill)
Indian recruiter placing US engineers: 'I have nothing to give these people besides the resume. All of these people look the same.' - commoditization already happened, AI made it visible
Careerspan competes to be the new source of differentiated signal in a market where old signals are worthless
Decision: 'Applicants are not auto-submitted to roles. They must explicitly click submit after seeing the opportunity' - protects candidate agency and validates response rates
Superposition wouldn't bring users or significant revenue but their logo creates a trust cascade - the brand IS the product, pure signaling economics
V's counter to 'you only have 500 users': 'Yes, but they're the right 500.' Inverts network effects - valuable because exclusive, not because big
Soho House model for talent platforms - capping signups to maintain exclusivity positioning vs. scale-driven flywheel thinking
Logan's biotech founder friend disrupted by going directly to morgues instead of middlemen - 'My middleman is charging me too much money' → go direct to source
Decision: Pursue both brand-name partnerships (Superposition) for credibility AND hungry individual recruiters for execution
Prioritize NYC channels: Harvard Club, Cornell Club, AI Circle via Albert - Logan primary owner with team all-in commitment
